mainEntity.name: How much does a 448DT (4.4 SDV8/TDV8) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 448DT SDV8 typically costs £6,000–£9,000 supply only, varying by specification and rebuild quality. Used units can be found from £3,500–£6,000, but provenance is essential. Fitting adds £1,200–£2,500 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the 448DT (4.4 SDV8/TDV8) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 448DT is widely regarded as the most durable of Land Rover's modern V8 diesels (4.0★). Its robust bottom-end design lacks the catastrophic failure modes of the smaller V6 units. The main watchpoints are oil cooler leaks and coolant crossover pipe corrosion.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common 448DT failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oil cooler leaks in the V of the engine and coolant crossover pipe failure are the most common issues — typically occurring at 100,000+ miles. Alternator failure is also reported due to oil contamination from cooler leaks. No catastrophic bottom-end design flaws are known.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the 448DT?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 448DT powers the Range Rover (L405) SDV8/TDV8 from 2012–2020, the Range Rover Sport (L494) SDV8 from 2013–2020, and the Discovery 4 (L319) SDV8 from 2014–2016.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover 448DT the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 448DT is shared with Jaguar fitments in the XJ (X351) and XF (X260). The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a 448DT typically takes 18–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Land Rover and Jaguar applications; labour time varies by engine bay access and subframe design.
